T.data_notes: Mock data. Primarily consumer/enterprise telecom. FirstNet is the main DoD-adjacent franchise (nationwide first-responder network). Government is ~8% of revenue; DoD specifically ~3%. High legacy debt from DirecTV acquisition (now divested). Competitive moat narrowing vs. Verizon and cable. Individual DoD contracts are immaterial to investment thesis. Price ~$23 approx mid-2025.

TMUS.data_notes: Mock data. T-Mobile Secure Federal Operations is their DoD/government subsidiary. Government revenue is a small share of total. Sprint merger integration substantially complete. Network infrastructure moat exists but competition from AT&T and Verizon is intense. Individual DoD contracts are not material to investment thesis. Price ~$165 approx mid-2025.

ACM.data_notes: Mock data. Formerly URS Group (acquired 2014) and now includes legacy URS and AECOM entities. Heavily government-focused engineering and construction services. DoD includes environmental remediation, base construction, infrastructure hardening. Spun off Amentum (management services) in 2023. Low margins typical of engineering services. Backlog provides meaningful revenue visibility. Price ~$86 approx mid-2025.

TTEK.data_notes: Mock data. Focused on environmental and engineering services for US government, DoD, and international clients. 'EC' subsidiary handles DoD environmental remediation (FUDS — Formerly Used Defense Sites program). Clean balance sheet relative to peers. Better margins than AECOM due to higher-value consulting mix. Meaningful government revenue concentration. Price ~$98 approx mid-2025.

J.data_notes: Mock data. Formerly Jacobs Engineering Group. Government/defense services is the core business — DoD, NASA, DHS, EPA. Rebranded as Jacobs Solutions after divesting lower-margin construction operations. Backlog provides solid forward visibility. 'Jacobs Government Services Company' is the primary DoD contracting entity. Strong position in critical infrastructure and national security programs. Price ~$101 approx mid-2025.

VZ.data_notes: Mock data. Primarily consumer/enterprise telecom. Government/DoD revenue is a small fraction of total. Network infrastructure moat but highly capital-intensive. Individual DoD contracts are not material to investment thesis. Price ~$42 approx mid-2025.

GD.data_notes: Mock data — verify via 10-K. Price ~$288 approx mid-2025.

HII.data_notes: Mock data — verify via 10-K. Newport News and Ingalls are sole US suppliers of nuclear carriers and amphibious warships. Price ~$242 approx mid-2025.

LDOS.data_notes: Mock data — verify via 10-K. Price ~$167 approx mid-2025.

PLTR.data_notes: Mock data. High valuation is the dominant risk. Revenue growth ~27% YoY. Government revenue concentration creates both stability and exposure. Price ~$80 approx mid-2025.

BAH.data_notes: Mock data. High P/B from leveraged recaps. High ROE is partly leverage-driven. Strong clearance/IC relationships are a moat component. Price ~$140 approx mid-2025.

KTOS.data_notes: Mock data. Kratos is a high-growth lower-tier defense tech company. Sole-source drone work provides moat element but profitability is still limited. Valuation is speculative. Price ~$35 approx mid-2025.

HUM.data_notes: Mock data. TRICARE contract is highly visible, durable government revenue. DoD % is relatively small vs total revenue; Medicare Advantage is core. Medical cost pressures are significant risk. Price ~$280 approx mid-2025.

BWXT.data_notes: Mock data. Sole supplier of naval nuclear reactor components and fuel. Extraordinarily durable moat — no practical substitute exists. High P/B from leverage. ROE inflated by leverage and buybacks. Price ~$113 approx mid-2025.

LMT.data_notes: Mock data. F-35 program creates massive long-term backlog. THAAD is a national security critical program. Some F-35 execution risk and C-130 maturity drag. Price ~$463 approx mid-2025.

NOC.data_notes: Mock data. B-21 sole-source position is a generational moat. Space Systems and Mission Systems provide additional durable program exposure. Price ~$468 approx mid-2025.

TMO.data_notes: Mock data. Very wide moat in life sciences tools. DoD CBRN biodefense work is small but growing. Primary thesis is commercial life sciences, not defense. Price ~$581 approx mid-2025.

CRWD.data_notes: Mock data. Strong product, expanding DoD footprint. Falcon platform stickiness creates moat. Valuation requires near-perfect execution and is extremely sensitive to growth deceleration. July 2024 outage is a reputational and legal overhang. Price ~$388 approx mid-2025.

PSN.data_notes: Mock data. Growing electronic warfare and directed energy exposure is differentiating. Infrastructure and critical mission work adds durability. Valuation relatively elevated for profitability level. Price ~$91 approx mid-2025.

RKLB.data_notes: Mock data. Company is unprofitable. Neutron development is capital intensive. Electron has established track record but faces competition from SpaceX. Valuation is speculative and requires growth realization. Price ~$26 approx mid-2025.

SAIC.data_notes: Mock data. Deep government IT embedding and recompete track record. Margins are thin (cost-plus dominant). Leverage is elevated from acquisitions. Price ~$119 approx mid-2025.

RTX.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Raytheon segment ~90% DoD; Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney blend down the total. Patriot, Tomahawk, SM-series provide sole-source moat. F135 engine partner. Approx DoD% ~62%, backlog ~$205B FY2024.

BA.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. BDS (Defense/Space/Security) ~40% of revenue; BCA commercial dominates backlog. Significant execution risk: quality control issues, labor disruption, and multi-year cash burn. Moat is narrowing — lost T-7, KC-46 problems, F-15 near end of production. Earnings_stability is low due to 2019-2023 net losses.

LHX.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Communications, electronic warfare, ISR, night vision. Merger of L3 + Harris 2019. High clearance/classified concentration. Moat: incumbency in tactical comms and EW pods. ~$21B revenue FY2024.

TXT.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Bell Textron (~60% DoD) is offset by commercial aviation (Cessna/Beechcraft), industrial (Kautex), and finance. Textron Aviation is commercial dominant. V-22 partner. FLRAA (V-280 Valor) award provides long-duration Bell exposure.

CACI.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Intelligence community and DoD IT services, cyber, and C2. Clearance-based moat: significant portion of work is TS/SCI-classified, creating real switching costs. ~$7.7B revenue FY2024. Strong organic growth track record.

AVAV.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Raven, Puma, Switchblade UAS are DoD sole-source programs. Ukraine demand drove significant revenue acceleration. High DoD concentration creates both durability and budget risk. ~$700M revenue; small-cap risk for position sizing.

PANW.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Platform consolidation strategy creating high switching costs — moat is widening. Government/DoD exposure is growing (Cortex XDR, XSIAM authorized at DoD) but primary thesis is commercial enterprise. Valuation requires strong execution.

KBR.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Government Services segment dominates; includes DoD logistics, science/space (NASA), and intel community. Sustainable Technology Solutions is commercial but growing. Strong pipeline in UK defence market. ~$7.8B revenue FY2024.

VVX.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Formed 2022 from Vectrus + Vertex Aerospace merger. Aviation sustainment, base operations, logistics. Virtually 100% US government revenue. Margins are thin (cost-plus dominant). High leverage from merger. ~$4B revenue. Not sole-source.

AMTM.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Spun from AECOM in 2023, became independent public company 2024. Management and operations services, environmental, intelligence. ~$13B revenue estimate. Very limited earnings history as standalone. High leverage from spin. Needs several years of clean public reporting before reliable fundamentals available.

FLR.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Mission Solutions segment handles DoD (Fluor Marine Propulsion naval nuclear JV, environmental remediation). Energy Solutions is the larger commercial segment. Significant execution risk on fixed-price contracts in recent years — accounting charges have dented track record. ~$16B revenue.

GE.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Post-Vernova spin (2024), GE is purely aerospace. Jet engines are a duopoly (GE + P&W/RTX). Military: F414, F110, T700, F404. Commercial: LEAP, GE9X. Enormous order backlog provides 5+ year revenue visibility. Wide moat from engine certification economics. ~$38B revenue FY2024.

TDG.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. ~75% of revenue is sole-source proprietary aftermarket parts. 80%+ gross margins on many components. DoD is a significant customer but commercial MRO drives pricing power. Serial acquirer of niche aerospace component businesses. Very high leverage by design (private equity heritage). ~$7.9B revenue FY2024.

HEI.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. FAA-PMA (Parts Manufacturer Approval) aftermarket parts provide niche moat — approved alternatives to OEM parts at 40-60% discount. Profitable every year since IPO 1990. Electronic Technologies Group serves defense/space. Smaller DoD exposure than TDG. ~$3.8B revenue FY2024.

CW.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Defense electronics, sensors, and naval systems. Naval valves and pumps for nuclear-powered ships provide specialized sole-source elements. Defense Industrial Group handles flight test and ground vehicle electronics. ~$2.9B revenue FY2024. Specialist mid-cap sweet spot.

IBM.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Federal government is a significant IBM customer (mainframe, cloud, consulting). DoD uses IBM systems across classified and unclassified networks. Moat: mainframe lock-in for legacy workloads, but cloud/consulting is competitive. Large commercial base means individual DoD contracts are not material to thesis. ~$62B revenue FY2024.

OLN.data_notes: Estimates — verify via 10-K. Winchester (ammunition) is the DoD-relevant segment. Chemical manufacturing (chlorine/caustic soda) is the larger business. Winchester has a long Army contract for small-caliber ammunition and Lake City management contract. DoD% is approximately the Winchester contribution (~$1.5B of $7.2B total revenue). Commodity chemical cycles drive most of earnings volatility.

ACN.data_notes: Mock data. AFS is subsidiary. DoD revenue is small relative to Accenture's total. Primary thesis is commercial consulting/technology, not defense. Price ~$333 approx mid-2025.
